techniques.name: plan-diff-apply-verify-report
techniques.summary: Safe workflow for agent-driven changes using explicit planning, scoped diffs, explicit validation, and concise reporting.
techniques.one_line_intent: Reduce unsafe, opaque, or non-reviewable agent changes by requiring a visible workflow before and...
techniques.use_when_short: Use when agent-assisted code changes or infrastructure or configuration edits.
techniques.do_not_use_short: Avoid when trivial wording fixes with no meaningful operational consequence or urgent manual intervention where immediate human action matters more...
techniques.inputs_short: Needs target goal; touched files or operational surfaces; intended validation method.
techniques.outputs_short: Produces explicit plan; scoped diff; applied change.
techniques.core_contract_short: Core contract: changes must stay reviewable by another human or agent; validation must be named explicitly, not implied.
techniques.main_risk_short: Main risk: weak or symbolic validation creates a false sense of safety around the applied change.
techniques.validation_short: Validate by checking a concrete plan exists before apply; the diff stays inside the declared scope; at least one explicit validation method...

techniques.name: source-of-truth-layout
techniques.summary: Repository document role separation pattern that keeps status, plans, history, decisions, and run instructions in distinct canonical homes.
techniques.one_line_intent: Reduce documentation drift by giving each recurring kind of project information one canonical document...
techniques.use_when_short: Use when multi-document repositories that mix planning, execution, history, and operations or solo+AI or human+agent projects where many edits happen...
techniques.do_not_use_short: Avoid when very small repositories where one short README is enough or projects that already use an external system as...
techniques.inputs_short: Needs current repository document set; recurring information classes such as status, plans, run history, decisions, and commands; update...
techniques.outputs_short: Produces explicit document role map; update-routing rules; lightweight entrypoint docs.
techniques.core_contract_short: Core contract: each recurring information class has one canonical home; each meaningful change routes to exactly one primary document first.
techniques.main_risk_short: Main risk: the role map drifts after repo evolution, so real updates no longer route to one canonical home.
techniques.validation_short: Validate by checking each major document has an explicit role; a sample change can be routed to one primary document without ambiguity...

techniques.name: contract-first-smoke-summary
techniques.summary: Runnable smoke pattern where each smoke path emits a machine-readable summary that becomes the primary validation contract.
techniques.one_line_intent: Make smoke checks reviewable, automatable, and reusable by treating the machine-readable summary as the...
techniques.use_when_short: Use when repositories with runnable smoke scripts or operational probes or CI pipelines that need stable pass or fail signals.
techniques.do_not_use_short: Avoid when one-off manual checks with no need for repeatability or cases where a full test suite already provides the...
techniques.inputs_short: Needs a runnable smoke entrypoint; expected output artifacts; explicit success and failure conditions.
techniques.outputs_short: Produces smoke run artifacts; one machine-readable summary file; explicit ok or error status.
techniques.core_contract_short: Core contract: each smoke path produces one machine-readable summary; the summary contains an explicit success or error status.
techniques.main_risk_short: Main risk: summaries collapse to a bare error signal without enough observed context to diagnose what failed.
techniques.validation_short: Validate by checking the summary file is always written on success and is written on failure whenever possible; the summary is valid...

techniques.name: intent-plan-dry-run-contract-chain
techniques.summary: Safe workflow that normalizes intent into a traceable plan, validates it with dry-run, and enforces contract checks before any real execution path exists.
techniques.one_line_intent: Turn high-level intent into a traceable, reviewable, dry-run-only chain that emits explicit artifacts and...
techniques.use_when_short: Use when automation systems that start from intent-like inputs or agent workflows that need a reviewable planning layer before execution.
techniques.do_not_use_short: Avoid when simple scripts that do not need an intent abstraction or systems that already execute real actions directly without...
techniques.inputs_short: Needs a stable intent payload; an adapter or normalizer that turns intent into a plan artifact; a dry-run-only...
techniques.outputs_short: Produces normalized plan artifact with routing metadata; dry-run execution artifacts; chain summary or equivalent linking intent, plan, and...
techniques.core_contract_short: Core contract: the adapter does not perform real input events or equivalent side effects; the planning step emits a plan...
techniques.main_risk_short: Main risk: dry-run artifacts look complete but do not reflect real execution constraints, creating false confidence.
techniques.validation_short: Validate by checking the intent payload is accepted or rejected by explicit normalization rules; the normalized plan is written as an artifact...

techniques.name: new-intent-rollout-checklist
techniques.summary: Checklist for safely adding a new intent type to an intent-plan-dry-run chain without contract drift.

techniques.use_when_short: Use when an automation chain already exists and the rollout is extending that shared chain, not redesigning it or one...
techniques.do_not_use_short: Avoid when there is no stable intent-chain contract yet or the rollout path performs real side effects before dry-run validation.

techniques.core_contract_short: Core contract: one canonical fixture exists for each new intent rollout; the rollout path stays dry-run only.
techniques.main_risk_short: Main risk: fixture drift makes the rollout look green while real inputs have already diverged.
techniques.validation_short: Validate by checking the canonical fixture exists and matches the new intent contract; the smoke path runs the new intent through the...

techniques.name: latest-alias-plus-history-copy

techniques.validation_short: Validate by checking the observational mode always publishes machine-readable output; the same summary contract is still readable after strict mode is introduced...

techniques.name: required-vs-optional-source-rendering

techniques.name: tdd-slice
